claim_support.reason: Material claims regarding stock performance (82% YTD, 27% five-session gain, 36% single-day jump, S&P 100 14% YTD) are not supported by visible evidence text or calculation IDs. While financial metrics are well-supported by structured calculations, the key investment narrative drivers (stock momentum) lack grounding in the provided evidence pack.

numerical_accuracy.reason: Financial statement numbers (Revenue, Margins, Cash Flow, Balance Sheet) are numerically accurate and match the provided calculation objects and evidence chunks precisely. Stock performance numbers are unverified and contain internal inconsistencies, preventing a perfect score.

citation_precision.reason: Citations provided for stock performance claims (e.g., [5cd3212e...], [883539ed...]) do not contain the specific percentage data (82%, 27%, 36%) in the visible text. While the IDs exist, they fail to substantiate the specific quantitative claims made in the prose.

temporal_validity.reason: All news sources and financial periods align with the report As-of date of 2025-09-15. Q1 2026 financials are appropriate for a September 2025 report (Oracle FY ends May 31). No post-as-of violations detected.

consistency.reason: Internal contradiction between '27% gain over the five sessions leading into September 15' (Recent Developments Para 2) and '36% single-day share increase after earnings' (Recent Developments Para 4). A 36% single-day gain within a 5-day window makes a total 5-day gain of only 27% mathematically inconsistent without significant offsetting losses not mentioned.
